era_context: The late 10th century CE. The Byzantine Empire under Basil II was expanding militarily in the Balkans. The Fatimid Caliphate was at the height of its power under Al-Aziz. Western Europe was in the High Middle Ages under the Ottonian dynasty. The Northern Song Dynasty was consolidating control in China under Emperor Taizong. The Khitan Liao Dynasty dominated the north. Islamic Spain was fragmenting. The Viking Age was in its final phase, with Scandinavian kingdoms consolidating.

events.title: Basil II's Balkan Campaigns
events.description: Byzantine Emperor Basil II continued his military campaigns in the Balkans in 995, systematically conquering Bulgarian territories and consolidating Byzantine control. Basil's campaigns were characterized by military innovation and ruthless tactics. By 995, he had made significant progress in subduing Bulgarian resistance, though the final conquest would not be completed until 1018. These campaigns were part of Basil's broader strategy to restore Byzantine hegemony in the Balkans and eliminate the Bulgarian Empire as an independent power.
events.certainty_note: Basil II's Balkan campaigns are well-attested, but exact yearly chronology is sometimes uncertain.

events.title: Al-Aziz's Rule in Fatimid Egypt
events.description: Al-Aziz (r. 975–996) was ruling the Fatimid Caliphate in 995. Al-Aziz was a powerful and capable ruler who had expanded Fatimid territory and wealth. He was consolidating Fatimid control over Egypt, the Levant, North Africa, and parts of the Arabian Peninsula. The Fatimid state was at the height of its territorial extent and economic power. Al-Aziz was also known for his military campaigns and his patronage of culture and learning.

sources.name: Al-Maqrizi, Itti'az al-Hunafa bi-Akhbar al-A'imma al-Fatimiyyin

events.certainty_note: Al-Aziz's reign is well-documented in Islamic chronicles.

events.title: Otto III's Regency and Early Rule in the Holy Roman Empire
events.description: Otto III (r. 983–1002) was ruling the Holy Roman Empire in 995. He had been king since 983 but was still in his late teens and was beginning to assert more direct authority. Otto was attempting to consolidate his power and deal with rebellions in Saxony and other regions. His reign was characterized by intellectual interests and attempts to blend Christian and classical ideals. Otto was also developing his relationship with Pope Gregory V, his cousin.
events.certainty_note: Otto III's activities in 995 are documented in contemporary sources.

events.title: Emperor Taizong's Rule in Song China
events.description: Emperor Taizong (r. 976–997) was ruling the Northern Song Dynasty in 995. Taizong had consolidated Song power and was promoting civil service examinations and administrative reforms. The Song Dynasty was experiencing economic growth and technological advancement. Taizong was dealing with military pressure from the Khitan Liao Dynasty to the north. His reign was marked by capable administration and the development of a sophisticated bureaucracy.
events.certainty_note: Taizong's reign is well-documented in Chinese historical records.

events.title: Al-Mansur's Military Campaigns in Al-Andalus
events.description: Al-Mansur (Almanzor), a military general, was conducting military campaigns in Al-Andalus in 995. He was consolidating his power and conducting raids against Christian kingdoms in the north. Al-Mansur was effectively controlling much of the Umayyad Caliphate's military and political power, while the nominal caliph Hisham II was increasingly sidelined. His military campaigns were successful and expanded Umayyad territory, but his rise also marked the beginning of the decline of centralized Umayyad rule.
events.certainty_note: Al-Mansur's military campaigns are well-attested in Islamic sources, though exact dating is sometimes uncertain.

disconfirming_evidence: No significant disconfirming evidence identified for the events listed. However, the exact chronology of Basil II's individual campaigns in 995 is approximate. The political situation in Al-Andalus in 995 is complex; the exact timeline of Al-Mansur's consolidation of power is debated by modern scholars.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 995 CE is moderate in quality. Byzantine, Islamic, and Western European sources are relatively abundant, but most are written decades or centuries after the events. Chinese sources are comprehensive. The major bias is toward elite political and military history. Economic and social history of non-elite populations is underrepresented. Sub-Saharan Africa, Southeast Asia, and the Americas have no written documentation for this year.

graph_edges.note: Al-Aziz's rule in 995 ended with his death in 996 and succession of Al-Hakim.
Taizong's rule in 995 continued until his death in 997 and succession of Zhenzong.
